input.background: The probability of failure of a medical device when it is tested is equal to 0.05. Assume independence of tests.
input.question: How can the memoryless property of the geometric distribution be shown?

output.answer: This memoryless property can be shown as follows. We know that $$P(N>n)=P(N \geq n+1)=(1-p)^n$$ Thus, $$\begin{aligned} P(N \geq(n+s) \mid N>s) & =\frac{P(N \geq n+s)}{P(N>s)} \\ & =\frac{(1-p)^{n+s-1}}{(1-p)^s} \\ & =(1-p)^{n-1} \\ & =P(N \geq n) \end{aligned}$$

meta_info.chapter: Chapter 2: Distribution Functions and Its Applications
